title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Sharp questions Elizabeth Johnston at the dormitory, inquiring if she senses anything unusual at the location. When Sharp mentions that Sally Finch believes something is strange, Elizabeth dismisses the observation as American neurosis. Sharp uses flattery to engage Elizabeth, noting her intelligence relative to other residents like Nigel Chapman, Leonard Betts, and Valerie Hobhouse. Elizabeth maintains that the hostel is respectable and denies any subversive activities, suggesting instead that Celia’s death was a personal matter involving a romantic entanglement. Following the interview, Sharp informs Sergeant Cobbs of his intention to return the next day with a search warrant, suspecting Elizabeth is withholding information.

In a separate scene, Hercule Poirot receives a phone call from Mrs. Haberdasher, who reports that Inspector Sharp has returned to the hostel with a search warrant and that Mrs. Nikolai is distraught. Poirot realizes he erred by not previously requesting a chronological list of missing items. Mrs. Haberdasher explains that her previous list—which included a high heel, bracelet, diamond ring, powder compact, lipstick, stethoscope, boric acid, light bulbs, and canvas backpacks—was organized by perceived importance rather than time. Poirot instructs her to rewrite the list in the exact order the items went missing.

On Saturday morning, Inspector Sharp returns to Chestnut Street with a search warrant. He attempts to speak with Mrs. Nicolaitis, who protests the search, fearing the investigation will cause her students to leave and lead to her bankruptcy. Mrs. Nicolaitis insists that Celia's death was a suicide, while Sharp maintains it is a murder investigation.
